topic: Cursor vs Windsurf vs Antigravity 2026: Agentic IDE Showdown
keyword: cursor vs windsurf vs antigravity comparison

competitors.title: Cursor vs Windsurf vs Antigravity: AI IDE Comparison
key_points: All three IDEs are VS Code forks — extensions and settings transfer seamlessly
SWE-bench scores: Cursor ~77%, Antigravity 76.2%, Windsurf ~75%
Antigravity's Manager View runs up to 5 parallel agents; Cursor and Windsurf run 1 at a time
Cursor: $20/month Pro, $40/month Business; Windsurf: $15/month Pro, $30/month Teams; Antigravity: free preview, ~$20/month Pro expected
Cursor has the most mature agentic features (2+ years), Windsurf is best value at $15/month, Antigravity offers unique parallel agent orchestration
Antigravity has native Chrome integration for browser testing; Cursor and Windsurf have limited browser integration

competitors.title: Google Antigravity vs Cursor vs Windsurf: Best AI IDE 2026
key_points: Antigravity has documented critical security vulnerabilities: 5 vulnerabilities found within 24 hours of launch including remote code execution and data exfiltration
A developer's entire D: drive was deleted by Antigravity's Turbo mode in December 2025
Cursor holds SOC 2 Type II certification; Windsurf has SOC 2 Type II + FedRAMP High; Antigravity has no enterprise compliance
Cursor has 360,000+ paying customers; enterprise maturity is battle-tested
Antigravity's Manager View: spawn up to 5 AI agents working in parallel on separate workspaces — architecturally different from single-agent Cursor/Windsurf
Windsurf acquired by Cognition (Devin AI) in July 2025 for $250M after OpenAI's $3B deal collapsed and Google's $2.4B talent grab
All three are VS Code forks — switching is low-risk

competitors.title: Windsurf vs Cursor 2026: LogRocket #1, Pricing Backlash & Real Comparison
key_points: Windsurf hit #1 on LogRocket's AI Dev Tool Power Rankings in February 2026, dethroning Cursor; Antigravity took #2
Windsurf Wave 14 introduced Arena Mode (blind model comparison) — no competitor has this feature
Cursor's SWE-1.5 at 950 tok/s; Cursor Composer model is 4x faster than peers for agentic tasks
Cursor: up to 8 parallel agents with cloud VMs; Windsurf: multi-agent sessions with Git worktrees
Windsurf's pricing range ($0-$60) compares favorably to Cursor's ($0-$200) after Cursor's June 2025 pricing backlash
OpenAI's failed $3B bid for Windsurf; Google hired co-founders for $2.4B; Cognition acquired remaining IP for $250M within 72 hours
Windsurf leads on codebase understanding (Codemaps, Fast Context, Arena Mode); Cursor leads on agent parallelism and speed

competitors.title: Cursor AI Statistics 2026: Users, Revenue and Adoption
key_points: Cursor ARR exceeded $2.0 billion by February 2026, up from $1.0B in November 2025
Cursor raised $2.3B Series D at $29.3B valuation (November 2025)
Cursor has 1M+ daily active users and 50,000 businesses
Over half of the Fortune 500 uses Cursor (company claim, mid-2025)
84% of developers use or plan to use AI tools; 51% use AI daily (Stack Overflow 2025)
JetBrains AI Pulse: 69% developer awareness, 18% workplace usage for Cursor

angles: Security as the deciding factor: Antigravity's documented vulnerabilities vs. Cursor/Windsurf's enterprise compliance

Corporate drama impact: Windsurf's 3 ownership changes in 2025 (OpenAI, Google, Cognition) vs. Cursor's steady growth
Value proposition gap: Windsurf at $15/month delivers comparable features to Cursor at $20/month — is the premium worth it?

VS Code fork advantage: All three are VS Code forks, making switching low-risk — developers can try all three
